existing_Entity_Description: Fort St. John is a municipality in Peace Region & Northeast, British Columbia, Canada, within the Peace River. As of the 2021 Canadian Census, Fort St. John had a population of 21,465. The community is situated in BC's energy heartland — oil, gas, and the spirit of the northeast frontier. cyclical but high-value market driven by energy sector activity. The region features long, cold winters and short, warm summers — northern continental climate.

REGION_BUNDLE.climate: BC's only true prairie-continental climate, east of the Rockies: winters can hit -40°C with chinook swings, summers are long-daylight and warm; permafrost-free but deep-frost construction rules apply.
REGION_BUNDLE.terrain: The Peace River country: rolling farmland (BC's only large-scale grain belt) cut by the deep Peace River valley, with the Rockies' foothills to the west and boreal muskeg north of Fort Nelson.
REGION_BUNDLE.economy: Natural gas (the Montney formation makes Fort St. John BC's energy capital), the Site C dam project, grain and canola farming around Dawson Creek — Mile 0 of the Alaska Highway — and forestry.
REGION_BUNDLE.building_stock: Farmhouses, energy-boom subdivisions in Fort St. John and Dawson Creek, worker housing, and rural shops/quonsets; buildings engineered for extreme cold.
